purpose: Persists chat conversations as JSON files in `{ROOT}/conversations/`. Used by the in-app Claude/Codex chat UIs (ClaudeView, ChatInstanceView) to save full message histories, list/load/rename/delete past sessions, and filter by dashboardId + surface ('chat' vs 'code'). Each conversation is a single JSON document {id, name, created, updated, dashboardId, surface, messages[]}.
gotchas: Surface filtering is asymmetric for legacy entries: conversations missing the `surface` field default to 'code' for filtering. Passing surface='chat' EXCLUDES legacy entries even though they may have originated from the chat UI before the field was added.
saveConversation does NOT auto-default `surface` — callers must pass it explicitly. Forgetting to pass it on a new conversation will mean it filters as legacy/'code' on the next list.
ID convention is `conv_<Date.now()>` — two simultaneous createConversation() calls within the same ms would collide silently (last-write-wins).
All errors are swallowed and returned as `null` from listConversations / loadConversation — callers cannot distinguish 'file not found' from 'malformed JSON'.
patterns: One JSON file per conversation in CONVERSATIONS_DIR (no index file). listConversations re-reads every file on every call.
Sort newest-first by `updated` (ISO string compared via Date.parse).
Optional dashboardId+surface filtering happens server-side rather than client-side.
conventions: Always touches `updated` to new Date().toISOString() on save.
Functions return either a data object or `{error: message}` — never throw.
